templates.fixed_prompt_action:
You are controlling one warehouse agent.
Choose a suitable Steps value.
Fewer LLM calls improve performance, so use a larger Steps value when the plan is stable and unlikely to need revision.
But do not overcommit: use a smaller Steps value when the environment may change soon, coordination may be needed, battery risk is rising, or urgent actions may appear.
Step: {step}
Agent: agent_{agent_id} ({agent_type})
Self state: {self_state}
Requested shelves:
{requested_shelves}
All agents:
{all_agents}
Candidate actions:
{candidate_actions}
Respond in plain text only:
Reasoning: <short>
Action: <action_id>
Steps: <1..{max_hold_steps}>

But do not overcommit: use a smaller Steps value when the environment may change soon, coordination may be needed, battery risk is rising, or urgent actions may appear.

templates.message_or_action:
You are controlling one warehouse agent.
At this step you must choose exactly one primary decision:
- ACTION: take one valid environment action now.
- MESSAGE: send one coordination message to another agent now.
Choose MESSAGE when communication is more valuable than immediate movement, such as support-request timing, plan-intent coordination, battery coordination, or shelf handoff coordination.
Choose ACTION when immediate execution is more valuable than messaging.
If you choose ACTION, the action id must be one of the valid candidate action ids.
If you choose MESSAGE, do not invent an action id; leave the Action line blank.
Choose a suitable Steps value.
Fewer LLM calls improve performance, so use a larger Steps value when the chosen action is stable and unlikely to need revision.
But do not overcommit: use a smaller Steps value when message handling, support timing, battery changes, or environment changes may require a quick replanning response.
Step: {step}
Agent: agent_{agent_id} ({agent_type})
Self state: {self_state}
Pending messages for you:
{inbox_messages}
Current priority message:
{active_message}
Recently handled messages:
{recently_handled_messages}
Requested shelves:
{requested_shelves}
All agents:
{all_agents}
Candidate actions:
{candidate_actions}
Respond in plain text only.
Use EXACTLY this field order so the output can be parsed:
Reasoning: <1-2 short sentences on why you chose ACTION or MESSAGE>
Decision: <ACTION or MESSAGE>
Decision Detail: <why this is better than the other option right now>
Focus Message: <message_id to work on now, or blank if no message is relevant>
Message Status: <KEEP, ACTIVE, or HANDLED>
Status Detail: <why the chosen message should stay pending, become active, or be marked handled>
Action: <valid action_id for ACTION, blank for MESSAGE>
Action Detail: <why this specific action fits the current state, or write none for MESSAGE>
Steps: <1..{max_hold_steps}>
To: <agent_id as integer, or blank for ACTION>
Message: <short coordination message, or blank for ACTION>
Message Detail: <why this specific message helps coordination now, or write none for ACTION>
Rules:
- Output exactly one block with the twelve fields above.
- Do not add bullets, code fences, JSON, or extra commentary.
- If a pending message should remain in progress, set Focus Message to its id and Message Status to ACTIVE.
- If a pending message has been fully dealt with or is no longer needed, set Focus Message to its id and Message Status to HANDLED.
- If no pending message should change state this turn, leave Focus Message blank and set Message Status to KEEP.
- For ACTION: fill Action and Steps, leave To and Message blank, set Message Detail to none.
- For MESSAGE: fill To and Message, leave Action blank, set Action Detail to none.
- Keep Message concise and operational.

reflection_settings.system_goal: Increase total shelf deliveries by using timely AGV/Picker support messaging.
